name: Small Business Innovation and Economic Security Act
summary: This act extends the SBIR and STTR programs through 2031, adds new security screening requirements for award applicants, creates a new Strategic Breakthrough Funding mechanism for large Phase II awards, reduces administrative burdens on small businesses, improves Phase III commercialization, and enhances data collection and workforce training.
affects: Small businesses applying for federal research and development awards under SBIR and STTR programs

children.name: Research Security Enhancements
children.summary: Adds new security risk evaluation requirements for SBIR and STTR award applicants, including due diligence checks against multiple federal watchlists and coordination with intelligence agencies.
children.affects: Small businesses seeking SBIR and STTR awards from federal agencies

children.name: SBIR Security Screening (Section 9(g))
children.summary: Requires federal agencies to evaluate whether small business applicants present security risks through due diligence, disclosures, and coordination with intelligence and law enforcement. Denial notifications must be provided when possible without compromising national security.
children.affects: SBIR program applicants

children.name: STTR Security Screening (Section 9(o))
children.summary: Requires federal agencies to evaluate whether small business applicants present security risks through due diligence, disclosures, and coordination with intelligence and law enforcement. Denial notifications must be provided when possible without compromising security.
children.affects: STTR program applicants

children.name: Due Diligence Requirements (Section 9(vv))
children.summary: Expands due diligence requirements to assess cybersecurity practices, patent analysis, employee analysis, foreign ownership, foreign affiliations, investment relationships, technology licensing agreements, and business relationships with foreign countries of concern.
children.affects: Small businesses seeking SBIR and STTR awards

children.name: GAO Study Extension
children.summary: Extends the GAO study on SBIR and STTR programs from 3 years to 8 years after the 2022 extension act.
children.affects: Congressional oversight of SBIR and STTR programs

children.name: Strategic Breakthrough Funding
children.summary: Creates a new funding mechanism allowing federal agencies with large SBIR budgets to award up to $30 million per small business for Phase II projects meeting critical technology needs, with matching funds requirements and streamlined contracting.
children.affects: Established small businesses with prior Phase II awards in critical technology areas

children.name: Strategic Breakthrough Allocation Definition
children.summary: Allows federal agencies with SBIR budgets over $100 million to set aside up to 0.50 percent of extramural R&D budget for strategic breakthrough awards starting in fiscal year 2026.
children.affects: Federal agencies with large SBIR programs

children.name: Award Requirements
children.summary: Awards up to $30 million per small business with 48-month performance period, requiring prior Phase II experience, 100% matching funds from private capital or other government programs, market-validated technology, and for DoD awards, specific acquisition readiness commitments.
children.affects: Small businesses with proven track records seeking large-scale federal R&D funding

children.name: Commercialization Readiness Program Updates
children.summary: Requires DoD to integrate strategic breakthrough awardees into programming and budgeting processes and provide congressional information on SBIR and STTR integration in budget rollouts.
children.affects: DoD SBIR program managers and congressional oversight committees

children.name: Congressional Briefing Requirements
children.summary: Requires federal agencies to brief Congress within 60 days on plans to use strategic breakthrough authority, with recurring briefings until procedures are finalized.
children.affects: Congressional committees on small business and science and technology

children.name: Sunset Provision
children.summary: Strategic breakthrough funding authority expires on September 30, 2031, with provisions reverting to pre-amendment status.
children.affects: Future small businesses and federal agencies after 2031

children.name: Administrative Burden Reduction
children.summary: Requires federal agencies to set limits on how many SBIR and STTR proposals a small business can submit per year, with waiver provisions for urgent mission-critical topics.
children.affects: Small businesses submitting multiple SBIR and STTR proposals annually

children.name: Proposal Submission Limits
children.summary: Starting fiscal year 2027, each federal agency must set annual limits on Phase I and Phase II proposal submissions per small business, using fiscal year, solicitation, or topic-based methods.
children.affects: Small businesses that frequently submit multiple SBIR and STTR proposals

children.name: Waiver Authority
children.summary: Allows agency directors to grant waivers for time-sensitive urgent topics, limited to 5% of topics per year, with written justification and 15-day approval requirement from senior officials. Waiver authority cannot be delegated.
children.affects: Federal agencies with urgent mission-critical research needs

children.name: Reporting Requirements
children.summary: Requires agencies to report to Congress on limit methodologies and waiver grants within 30 days, including historical impact data and written justifications.
children.affects: Congressional oversight committees and federal agency SBIR program offices

children.name: Phase III Commercialization Improvements
children.summary: Enhances training, education, and procedural support to increase federal procurement of technologies developed through SBIR and STTR programs.
children.affects: Federal contracting officers and small businesses with commercializable SBIR and STTR technologies

children.name: Acquisition Workforce Training
children.summary: Requires the SBA Administrator to establish training for federal contracting officers on Phase III awards, including program missions, data rights, and sole source award execution, in coordination with DoD and GSA.
children.affects: Federal contracting officers and agency acquisition workforce

children.name: Procurement Center Representative Directives
children.summary: Requires SBA procurement center representatives to advocate for maximum practicable use and transition of SBIR and STTR technologies to Phase III awards.
children.affects: SBA procurement center representatives and small businesses with commercializable technologies

children.name: Phase III Award Simplification
children.summary: Requires federal agencies to develop simplified and standardized procedures and model contracts for all SBIR phases, with clear guidance on information requirements for Phase III eligibility.
children.affects: Small businesses seeking Phase III awards and federal contracting officers

children.name: Technical and Business Assistance Improvements
children.summary: Reforms how small businesses access technical and business assistance, allowing more flexibility in using funds for staff, training, cybersecurity, and foreign involvement screening.
children.affects: SBIR and STTR award recipients seeking technical and business assistance

children.name: Assistance Authorization Changes
children.summary: Changes from agency-selected vendors to recipient-selected assistance, adds cybersecurity assistance and foreign involvement screening, and allows use of funds for staff hiring, augmentation, or training.
children.affects: SBIR and STTR award recipients

children.name: Funding Levels
children.summary: Sets Phase I assistance at up to $6,500 per project and Phase II at up to $50,000 per project, included in or added to award amounts at agency discretion.
children.affects: SBIR and STTR Phase I and Phase II award recipients

children.name: I-Corps Participation
children.summary: Requires federal agencies with I-Corps programs to offer SBIR and STTR recipients the option to participate, with costs covered through various funding sources including SBIR and STTR program funds.
children.affects: SBIR and STTR award recipients interested in entrepreneurship training

children.name: Data Collection Improvements
children.summary: Enhances tracking and reporting of SBIR and STTR awards across federal databases to improve transparency and program evaluation.
children.affects: Researchers, policymakers, and oversight bodies tracking SBIR and STTR program outcomes

children.name: SBIR Database Expansion
children.summary: Adds new data fields to track whether awards are direct to Phase II, subsequent Phase II, strategic breakthrough, Phase III prime, or Phase III subcontract awards.
children.affects: Users of the SBIR award database

children.name: Federal Procurement Data System Updates
children.summary: Requires GSA to update procurement systems to track SBIR and STTR award designations, Phase III contracts, non-SBIR contracts using SBIR-funded technology, and link prior SBIR and STTR work through contract identification numbers.
children.affects: Federal procurement officials and program evaluators

children.name: Program Extension to 2031
children.summary: Extends authorization and various pilot programs for SBIR and STTR from 2025 to 2031, with carryover of fiscal year 2026 funds permitted into 2027.
children.affects: All current and future participants in SBIR and STTR programs

children.name: Core Program Authorization
children.summary: Extends SBIR and STTR program authorizations from September 30, 2025 to September 30, 2031.
children.affects: All federal agencies and small businesses participating in SBIR and STTR

children.name: Phase Flexibility Extension
children.summary: Extends direct to Phase II and STTR program flexibility until September 30, 2031, and adds NASA and Department of Energy to participating agencies.
children.affects: Small businesses seeking direct to Phase II awards and agencies with STTR programs

children.name: Multiple Pilot Program Extensions
children.summary: Extends commercialization readiness, accelerated awards, Phase 0, administrative assistance, increased minimum performance standards, commercialization assistance, due diligence, STTR military research institution participation, budget calculation, and Special Operations Command pilot programs to 2031.
children.affects: Participants in various SBIR and STTR pilot programs

children.name: Carryover Funds Provision
children.summary: Allows federal agencies to carry over unspent fiscal year 2026 SBIR and STTR funds into fiscal year 2027.
children.affects: Federal agencies with unspent SBIR and STTR allocations at end of fiscal year 2026

children.name: GAO Mandate Sunset
children.summary: Repeals the GAO mandate for annual SBIR and STTR reports under the 2012 National Defense Authorization Act.
children.affects: GAO and congressional oversight of SBIR and STTR programs
